unexpected_finds.description: The Sakai Cremona group Aut(D_6^{(1)}) ⋉ W((2A_1)^{(1)}) is STRICTLY LARGER than Okamoto's W^{aff}(B_2) by index 2; the bridge is an INJECTION of W^{aff}(B_2), NOT an isomorphism.
unexpected_finds.evidence: Phase C kernel/image analysis: image of φ = ⟨σ, ππ'⟩ ⋉ W((2A_1)^{(1)}) of index 2; cokernel Z/2 generated by π (one-sided leaf swap).
unexpected_finds.implication: The 'M6 Phase B.5 W cross-walk anchor' as originally framed (suggesting an isomorphism) is technically incorrect. The accurate statement is an INDEX-2 EMBEDDING, with the cokernel filled by an extra Bäcklund involution that lives outside W^{aff}(B_2) on the Okamoto side. CT v1.4 §3.5 amendment (G17 layer-separation, prompt 032) should reflect this nuance.

unexpected_finds.description: The cokernel-generating Bäcklund involution π has explicit Okamoto-side form (v_1, v_2) ↦ (−1−v_1, v_2). Although Okamoto 1987 §2.3 introduces y: v↦−v as an additional involution beyond G, π itself does not appear to be discussed there as a separate involution. (Whether π appears later in Okamoto §3 — symmetry of the Hamiltonian — was not investigated this session.)
unexpected_finds.evidence: verify_homomorphism.log line 41: π acting as (a_1,a_0,b_0,b_1) on Sakai side, equivalent to (v_1,v_2)↦(−1−v_1,v_2) on Okamoto side.
unexpected_finds.implication: Future session may want to scan Okamoto §3 for π or to register π as a SIARC-primary additional Bäcklund involution. (Operator decision.)

unexpected_finds.description: Okamoto's auxiliary involution y: v↦−v lies INSIDE the image of φ (as the Weyl element r_0 r'_0 = φ((s_1 s_2)²)). So y is NOT an external generator beyond W^{aff}(B_2); it is an internal element of order 2 in W^{aff}(B_2).
unexpected_finds.evidence: verify_homomorphism.log line 38; r_0 r'_0 acting on init_s = (v_1, 1−v_1, v_2, 1−v_2) = from_v(−v_1, −v_2).
unexpected_finds.implication: Okamoto's framing of y as a 'separate involution' (slot 07 §2.3) is consistent with our finding only because y is realised as a non-obvious word-of-length-4 in s_1, s_2 within G; the explicit identification y = (s_1 s_2)² is a (small) primary observation that may be of independent interest for the SIARC-authored short note (D-08 candidate).
